title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Hackett visits the home of Mrs. Rotten to conduct inquiries regarding a murder case involving her niece, Hilary (also known as Sheila) Webb. While waiting in the messy living room, Hackett observes letters addressed to Mrs. Rotten and Miss R.S. Webber. Mrs. Rotten explains that Hilary is currently working late for Professor Puddy, a man described as pleasant but lacking a sense of time.

Hackett discusses the upcoming trial, noting that Hilary only needs to state her discovery of the body. He mentions that the identity of the deceased is still unknown, though a business card found in the victim's pocket suggests the man may have been considering insurance. During the questioning, Hackett clarifies Hilary's full name as Rosalind Sheila Webb, though she prefers Sheila. Mrs. Rotten provides details about Hilary's previous life in London, noting she lived at 17 Caldington Grove near Fulham Road and worked for a real estate company called Hopgood and Trent before joining Cavendish Document Printing last November.

When Hackett asks if Hilary is an orphan, Mrs. Rotten confirms she is and that she is the child of Mrs. Rotten's sister. However, Mrs. Rotten claims she does not know the occupation of Hilary's father. Hackett suggests that Hilary may have been deliberately lured to the scene of the crime by someone with a grudge or someone attempting to frame her. He speculates the perpetrator could be a rejected suitor or a jealous former roommate from London. Mrs. Rotten defends Hilary's character, though she considers the possibility of a non-serious attempt to frame her.
